                        dropped_items_on_map.append((feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], char))
                        feature_type = f"dropped item '{char}'"
                    elif char == ' ': # empty space outside known areas
                        feature_type = "empty space"
                    # Any other characters not explicitly defined would remain 'unknown'

                    if is_adjacent_char:
                        dx = x_offset - visual_agent_x_on_map
                        dy = y_offset - visual_agent_y_on_map
                        move_dir = adjacent_directions.get((dx, dy))

                        if move_dir:
                            # Special handling for molten lava or other lethal features regardless of general classification (e.g. open floor)
                            is_lethal = False
                            feature_type_for_interact = feature_type # Default to general feature type

                            # Check if the current feature (char) is one we know to be lethal
                            if char == '}': # The map character '}' is identified as lethal as per world knowledge
                                is_lethal = True
                                feature_type_for_interact = "molten lava"
                                
                                # Add to adjacent zappable features list with danger assessment
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - molten lava) (safe solidification)"
                                ) # New line for zappable info

                            # The `.` open floor tile can also be lethal if zapped into due to reflection,
                            # so assess it here for zappable features. Note: This is *not* for immediate movement.
                            elif char in ('.', ':', 'F', '<', '>'): # Reflective surfaces
                                # This is for classifying for `zap` action, not for *movement* lethality
                                current_surface_type = ""
                                if char == '.': current_surface_type = "open floor"
                                elif char == ':': current_surface_type = "corridor"
                                elif char == 'F': current_surface_type = "fountain"
                                elif char == '<': current_surface_type = "stairs up"
                                elif char == '>': current_surface_type = "stairs down"
                                
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - {current_surface_type}) (lethal reflection risk)"
                                )
                            elif re.match(r"[a-z]", char) and char != '@': # Dropped items are also reflective
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - dropped item) (lethal reflection risk)"
                                )
                            elif char == '|' or char == '-': # Walls
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - wall) (no reflection risk)"
                                )
                            elif char == '+': # Closed doors
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - closed door) (no reflection risk)"
                                )
                            
                            if is_lethal: # Molten lava takes precedence
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"LETHAL ({feature_type_for_interact})", feature_coords)
                                lethal_adjacent_move_targets.append(feature_coords)
                                # For interactive features, specify zappability for molten lava
                                if char == '}':
                                    adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], "molten lava (lethal, zappable with cold wand, safe solidification)")
                                else:
                                    adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], feature_type_for_interact)
                            elif char == 'r':
                                monsters_within_striking_distance.append((feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], 'rat'))
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"ATTACK (rat monster)", feature_coords)
                                adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], "rat monster") # For interaction detail
                            elif char == '|' or char == '-': # Walls are impassable for movement
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"BLOCKED (wall)", feature_coords)
                                adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], "wall") # For interaction detail
                            elif char == '+': # Closed doors are impassable for movement (until opened)
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"BLOCKED (closed door)", feature_coords)
                                adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], "closed door") # For interaction detail
                            else: # Default safe for other passable terrain features like floor, corridor, stairs, fountain, and explicitly OPEN DOORS
                                if move_dir not in immediate_move_options: # Prioritize more specific statuses
                                    # Use the refined feature_type from above
                                    if char == '<': feature_type_for_interact = "stairs up"
                                    elif char == '>': feature_type_for_interact = "stairs down"
                                    elif char == '.': feature_type_for_interact = "open floor"
                                    elif char == 'F': feature_type_for_interact = "fountain"
                                    elif char == ':': feature_type_for_interact = "corridor"
                                    elif re.match(r"[a-z]", char) and char != '@': feature_type_for_interact = f"dropped item '{char}'"
                                    else: feature_type_for_interact = "open space" # fallback
                                    
                                    immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"SAFE ({feature_type_for_interact})", feature_coords)
                                    safe_adjacent_move_targets.append(feature_coords) # Moved this here to ensure it's only for SAFE moves
                                    adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], feature_type_for_interact)
            
            # --- First Visible Zap Targets Logic Start ---
            # Only calculate if the agent's visual position on the map is known
            if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                # Iterate through directions: N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW
                # The dx, dy tuples here correspond to (offset_x, offset_y) from agent's visual position
                direction_vectors = {
                    "north": (0, -1), "northeast": (1, -1), "east": (1, 0), "southeast": (1, 1),
                    "south": (0, 1), "southwest": (-1, 1), "west": (-1, 0), "northwest": (-1, -1)
                }
                
                temp_zap_obstacles = []
                # agent_central_coords (absolute or visual depending on is_absolute_mapping_possible)
                agent_central_coords = (agent_x, agent_y) if is_absolute_mapping_possible else (visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map)

                for dir_name, (dx_dir, dy_dir) in direction_vectors.items():
                    current_steps = 1
                    first_obstacle_info = None # (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, dist_manhattan)
                    beam_path_description = [] # To store details of what the beam passes through

                    while True:
                        check_visual_x = visual_agent_x_on_map + dx_dir * current_steps
                        check_visual_y = visual_agent_y_on_map + dy_dir * current_steps

                        # Check if within map bounds of the current snippet
                        if not (0 <= check_visual_y < map_height and 
                                0 <= check_visual_x < map_width and 
                                check_visual_y < len(map_lines) and 
                                check_visual_x < len(map_lines[check_visual_y])): 
                            # If out of map bounds, and no obstacle found, the beam goes into void, which is safe.
                            if not first_obstacle_info: # Only if nothing was hit yet, then the target is 'empty space (void)'
                                feature_coords_for_void = (check_visual_x + current_map_offset_x, check_visual_y + current_map_offset_y) if is_absolute_mapping_possible else (check_visual_x, check_visual_y)
                                current_dist, _ = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_central_coords[0], agent_central_coords[1], feature_coords_for_void[0], feature_coords_for_void[1])
                                # Use ' ' as char_at_pos for empty space (void)
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords_for_void, ' ', "empty space (void)", "no reflection risk", current_dist)
                            break # Out of bounds of the current map snippet or line too short

                        char_at_pos = map_lines[check_visual_y][check_visual_x]

                        # Determine feature coords (absolute or visual)
                        if is_absolute_mapping_possible:
                            feature_coords = (check_visual_x + current_map_offset_x, check_visual_y + current_map_offset_y)
                        else:
                            feature_coords = (check_visual_x, check_visual_y)

                        # Manhattan distance
                        distance_to_feature_manhattan, _ = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_central_coords[0], agent_central_coords[1], feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1])
                        
                        obstacle_type = "unknown"
                        zap_danger = "unknown"
                        
                        # Categorize the feature for beam interaction
                        if char_at_pos == '}':
                            obstacle_type = "molten lava"
                            zap_danger = "safe solidification"
                            if not first_obstacle_info:
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, distance_to_feature_manhattan)
                            break # Lava is always the primary target if encountered
                        elif char_at_pos in ('|', '-', '#', '+'): # Walls, corridor walls, closed doors are solid obstacles
                            obstacle_type = "solid obstacle (wall/closed door/corridor wall)"
                            zap_danger = "no reflection risk" # Safe to zap at solid objects
                            if not first_obstacle_info:
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, distance_to_feature_manhattan)
                            break # Solid objects block beam
                        elif char_at_pos in ('.', ':', 'F', '<', '>') or (re.match(r"[a-z]", char_at_pos) and char_at_pos not in ('@', 'r')): # Reflective surfaces, exlcuding agent and rat monsters
                            # Record what the beam passes through, but don't stop unless it's the first obstacle
                            current_surface_type = ""
                            if char_at_pos == '.': current_surface_type = "open floor"
                            elif char_at_pos == ':': current_surface_type = "corridor"
                            elif char_at_pos == 'F': current_surface_type = "fountain"
                            elif char_at_pos == '<': current_surface_type = "stairs up"
                            elif char_at_pos == '>': current_surface_type = "stairs down"
                            elif (re.match(r"[a-z]", char_at_pos) and char_at_pos != '@'): current_surface_type = "dropped item"
                            else: current_surface_type = "reflective surface" #fallback
                            
                            beam_path_description.append(f"({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char_at_pos} - {current_surface_type})")

                            if not first_obstacle_info: # If a reflective surface is the *first* thing hit
                                obstacle_type = current_surface_type # Set to the specific surface type
                                # Determine lethal reflection risk based on adjacency
                                zap_danger = "lethal reflection risk" if distance_to_feature_manhattan == 1 else "reflection risk (not immediately lethal)"
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, distance_to_feature_manhattan)
                                # For a purely reflective surface as target, the beam stops and reflects.
                                break 
                        elif char_at_pos == 'r': # Monsters are also reflective
                            obstacle_type = "rat monster"
                            zap_danger = "reflection risk (not immediately lethal)" # Mosters reflect but not immediately lethal, as per standard NetHack mechanics (can hit other targets, but not player directly from a single reflection)
                            if not first_obstacle_info:
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, distance_to_feature_manhattan)
                            break
                        elif char_at_pos == ' ': # Empty space / void within visible area, non-reflective and doesn't stop beam
                            beam_path_description.append(f"({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char_at_pos} - empty space)")
                            # If the beam reaches beyond the map or hits nothing, the 'void' is the target.
                            # We continue through empty space until a solid or reflective surface, or map edge.
                        
                        current_steps += 1
                        
                        if current_steps > max(map_height, map_width) + 2: # Heuristic to prevent infinite loops for edge cases
                             break

                    if first_obstacle_info:
                        f_coords, f_char, f_type, f_danger, f_dist = first_obstacle_info
                        desc = f"{dir_name}: {f_coords} ({f_char} - {f_type}) ({f_danger}, distance: {f_dist} steps)"
                        if beam_path_description:
                            # Filter out the final target from beam_path_description if it was added.
                            # The 'first_obstacle_info' already contains the terminal target.
                            # We only want to list intermediate reflective surfaces.
                            # Also filter out the STARTING point for the description. The first item in beam_path_description can be the target itself if it's adjacent.
                            final_target_str_part = f"({f_coords[0]}, {f_coords[1]}) ({f_char}"
                            # Filter out any path descriptions that are identical to the first obstacle info
                            filtered_beam_path = [
                                path_desc for path_desc in beam_path_description 
                                if not (final_target_str_part in path_desc and f_type in path_desc)
                            ]
                            
                            if filtered_beam_path:
                                desc += f" (Beam passes through {'; '.join(filtered_beam_path)} without reflection.)"
                        temp_zap_obstacles.append(desc)
            
            # Sort the distant zap obstacles for consistent output
            if temp_zap_obstacles:
                sorted_zap_targets = []
                direction_order_keys = ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]
                # Use the map coordinates or visual coordinates prefix once for the entire list
                prefix = f"First_visible_zap_targets (all coordinates are {coord_desc} - direction: (x,y) (char - type) (zap danger, distance)):"
                
                for d_key in direction_order_keys:
                    for item_str in temp_zap_obstacles:
                        if item_str.startswith(f"{d_key}:"):
                            sorted_zap_targets.append(item_str)
                extracted_info.append(f"{prefix} {' '.join(sorted_zap_targets)}.")
            # --- First Visible Zap Targets Logic End ---

            # After iterating through the map, explicitly state molten lava from accumulated list,
            # ensuring it takes precedence and is not overridden by general "open floor".
            if molten_lava:
                extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal) observed at {coord_desc}: {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                # Filter out lava from general open_floor list for cleaner, non-redundant output
                temp_open_floor = []
                # Rebuild open_floor to remove lava spots more carefully if they were initially categorized as open_floor before lava ID.
                # More direct approach: ensure points in molten_lava are not in open_floor list for reporting.
                unique_molten_lava_coords = set(molten_lava)
                open_floor = [coord for coord in open_floor if coord not in unique_molten_lava_coords]


            if walls:
                pass # Walls are covered by adjacent_interactive_features and first_visible_obstacle_for_distant_zap
            if open_floor:
                extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at {coord_desc}: {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")

            if fountains:    
                extracted_info.append(f"Fountain observed at {coord_desc}: {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                closest_fountain = None
                min_fountain_dist = float('inf')
                min_fountain_dir = ""
                for fx, fy in fountains:
                    if is_absolute_mapping_possible:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, fx, fy)
                    else:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map, fx, fy)
                    if dist < min_fountain_dist:
                        min_fountain_dist = dist
                        closest_fountain = (fx, fy)
                        min_fountain_dir = direction
                if closest_fountain:
                    # Added coord_desc to closest feature details
                    extracted_info.append(f"Closest fountain {coord_desc}: {closest_fountain} (distance: {min_fountain_dist} steps){min_fountain_dir}{' (relative to visual @)' if not is_absolute_mapping_possible and min_fountain_dir and coord_desc == 'visual coordinates relative to map snippet origin' else ''}.")

            if stairs_up:    
                extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at {coord_desc}: {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                closest_stairs_up = None
                min_stairs_up_dist = float('inf')
                min_stairs_up_dir = ""
                for sx, sy in stairs_up:
                    if is_absolute_mapping_possible:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, sx, sy)
                    else:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map, sx, sy)
                    if dist < min_stairs_up_dist:
                        min_stairs_up_dist = dist
                        closest_stairs_up = (sx, sy)
                        min_stairs_up_dir = direction
                if closest_stairs_up:
                    # Added coord_desc to closest feature details
                    extracted_info.append(f"Closest stairs up {coord_desc}: {closest_stairs_up} (distance: {min_stairs_up_dist} steps){min_stairs_up_dir}{' (relative to visual @)' if not is_absolute_mapping_possible and min_stairs_up_dir and coord_desc == 'visual coordinates relative to map snippet origin' else ''}.")

            if stairs_down:  
                extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at {coord_desc}: {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                closest_stairs_down = None
                min_stairs_down_dist = float('inf')
                min_stairs_down_dir = ""
                for sx, sy in stairs_down:
                    if is_absolute_mapping_possible:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, sx, sy)
                    else:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map, sx, sy)
                    if dist < min_stairs_down_dist:
                        min_stairs_down_dist = dist
                        closest_stairs_down = (sx, sy)
                        min_stairs_down_dir = direction
                if closest_stairs_down:
                    # Added coord_desc to closest feature details
                    extracted_info.append(f"Closest stairs down {coord_desc}: {closest_stairs_down} (distance: {min_stairs_down_dist} steps){min_stairs_down_dir}{' (relative to visual @)' if not is_absolute_mapping_possible and min_stairs_down_dir and coord_desc == 'visual coordinates relative to map snippet origin' else ''}.")

            if corridors:    
                extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at {coord_desc}: {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
            if doors_closed:
                extracted_info.append(f"Closed doors observed at {coord_desc}: {sorted(list(set(doors_closed)))}.")

            if monsters_rat: 
                extracted_info.append(f"All visible monsters (rat) observed at {coord_desc}: {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                
            # Consolidated for monster interaction - distinguishing attackable targets
            if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                # Format as (x, y) with monster type
                formatted_targets = [f"({mx}, {my})" for mx, my, _ in monsters_within_striking_distance]
                extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent monster attack targets ({coord_desc}): {sorted(formatted_targets)}.")

            if dropped_items_on_map:
                # Note: Char here is the map symbol, not necessarily the inventory item key
                extracted_info.append(f"Dropped items observed at {coord_desc}: {sorted([(x, y, char) for x, y, char in dropped_items_on_map])}.")
                closest_dropped_item = None
                min_item_dist = float('inf')
                min_item_dir = ""
                for ix, iy, item_char in dropped_items_on_map:
                    if is_absolute_mapping_possible:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, ix, iy)
                    else:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map, ix, iy)
                    if dist < min_item_dist:
                        min_item_dist = dist
                        closest_dropped_item = (ix, iy, item_char) # Store the actual char here
                        min_item_dir = direction
                if closest_dropped_item:
                    # Added coord_desc to closest feature details
                    extracted_info.append(f"Closest dropped item ({coord_desc}): {closest_dropped_item[:2]} (item '{closest_dropped_item[2]}', distance: {min_item_dist} steps){min_item_dir}{' (relative to visual @)' if not is_absolute_mapping_possible and min_item_dir and coord_desc == 'visual coordinates relative to map snippet origin' else ''}.")


            # Add safe and lethal adjacent moves
            if safe_adjacent_move_targets:
                extracted_info.append(f"Safe adjacent move targets ({coord_desc}): {sorted(list(set(safe_adjacent_move_targets)))}.")
            if lethal_adjacent_move_targets:
                extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Lethal (molten lava) adjacent move targets ({coord_desc}): {sorted(list(set(lethal_adjacent_move_targets)))}.")
            
            # Add immediate move options, sorted alphabetically
            
            # Order of moves for consistency: north, northeast, east, southeast, south, southwest, west, northwest
            sorted_move_options_str = []
            for move_dir in ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]:
                if move_dir in immediate_move_options:
                    status_full_text, coords = immediate_move_options[move_dir]
                    # Extract just the "SAFE" or "BLOCKED" or "LETHAL" part and the (feature_type)
                    status_type_match = re.search(r"^(SAFE|BLOCKED|LETHAL|ATTACK)\s+\((.*?)\)", status_full_text)
                    if status_type_match:
                        status_summary = f"{status_type_match.group(1)} ({status_type_match.group(2)})"
                    else:
                        status_summary = status_full_text # Fallback
                    
                    sorted_move_options_str.append(f"{move_dir}: {status_summary} at {coords}")
            
            if sorted_move_options_str:
                extracted_info.append(f"Immediate move options (all coordinates are {coord_desc}): {'; '.join(sorted_move_options_str)}.")


            # Adjacent interactive features for context or specific actions (e.g., kick, open)
            if adjacent_interactive_features:
                sorted_features_list = []
                # Keep consistent direction ordering
                for move_dir in ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]:
                    if move_dir in adjacent_interactive_features:
                        fx, fy, ftype = adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir]
                        sorted_features_list.append(f"{move_dir}: ({fx}, {fy}) - {ftype}")
                if sorted_features_list: # Ensure it's not empty after filtering
                    extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent interactive features (all coordinates are {coord_desc} - direction: (x,y) - type): {'; '.join(sorted_features_list)}.")

            # Add newly extracted adjacent zappable features
            if adjacent_zappable_features:
                # Sort by direction for consistent output order
                # Use a specific order for directions
                direction_order = ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]
                sorted_zappable_features = []
                # Iterate through preferred order and add to sorted list
                for d_ord in direction_order:
                    for feature_str in adjacent_zappable_features:
                        if feature_str.startswith(f"{d_ord}:"):
                            # The coord_desc is already included in the `prefix`
                            sorted_zappable_features.append(feature_str)
                # Remove duplicates while maintaining order (Python 3.7+ feature)
                sorted_zappable_features = list(dict.fromkeys(sorted_zappable_features))
                
                if sorted_zappable_features: # Ensure it's not empty, even after sorting
                    extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent zappable features (all coordinates are {coord_desc} - direction: (x,y) (char - type) (zap danger)): {'; '.join(sorted_zappable_features)}.")
                
    # Extract system feedback messages (e.g., "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry")
    # This specifically targets the "malformed action" feedback
    system_feedback_messages = re.findall(r"Your previous output did not contain a valid action\. Retry", observation_text)
    if system_feedback_messages:
        feedback_counts = {}
        for msg in system_feedback_messages:
            feedback_counts[msg] = feedback_counts.get(msg, 0) + 1
        
        formatted_feedback = []
        for msg, count in feedback_counts.items():
            formatted_feedback.append(f"'{msg}' (occurred {count} time(s))")
        
        extracted_info.append(f"System_feedback_message: {', '.join(formatted_feedback)}.")

    # Extract all generic messages first, then filter for inventory and prompts
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n(.*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\nAgent the \w+\s+St:|\nSt:|\nNo\s+Points|$)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1).strip()
    else:
        message_content_raw = "" 
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    interactive_prompt_str = None 
    
    # Check for death message again, independently of map context.
    death_message_pattern = re.compile(
        r"(?:No\s+Points\s+Name.*?)\n(?:.*?died in The Dungeons of Doom on\s+level \d+\.\s*)(Burned by molten lava\.|Killed by a bolt of cold\.|from (?:a|an|the) .*?\.|fell into a pit\.)",
        re.DOTALL
    )
    death_message_match = death_message_pattern.search(observation_text)

    if death_message_match:
        cause_of_death = death_message_match.group(1).strip()
        extracted_info.append(f"Death_message: Agent died. Cause: {cause_of_death}.")
        death_message_found = True
        agent_character_class = None # Clear class on death
        agent_current_title = None # Clear title on death
    else:
        general_death_message_match = re.search(r"(?:No\s+Points\s+Name.*?)\n(.*?died in.*?\.)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
        if general_death_message_match:
             extracted_info.append(f"Death_message: {general_death_message_match.group(1).strip()}.")
             death_message_found = True
             agent_character_class = None # Clear class on death
             agent_current_title = None # Clear title on death

    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(\s+\(\d+:\d+\))?(?=\.\s\s|\.$|\n|$)")
    interactive_prompt_pattern = re.compile(r"^(What do you want to ([a-z]+)\? \[[a-z?* ]+\])$", re.IGNORECASE)
    direction_prompt_pattern = re.compile(r"^\s*In what direction\?\s*$") # Added \s* for leading/trailing whitespace

    # Patterns to identify lines that should be excluded from general messages if a death occurred
    excluded_message_patterns = [
        re.compile(r"Your previous output did not contain a valid action\. Retry"),
        interactive_prompt_pattern,
        direction_prompt_pattern, # Now explicitly excluded
        re.compile(r"^\s*No\s+Points\s+Name\s+Hp\s+\[max\]\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*\d+\s+Agent-.*?died in The Dungeons of Doom on$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*level \d+\.\s+.*? Burned by molten lava\.\s+- \[(\d+)\]\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*level \d+\.\s+.*? Killed by a bolt of cold\.\s+- \[(\d+)\]\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*level \d+\.\s+.*? died in The Dungeons of Doom on\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*You made the top ten list!\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*Hp \[max\]\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*\d+\s+Agent-\w+-\w+-\w+-\w+ died in The Dungeons of Doom on$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*--More--\s*$"), # Exclude --More--
        re.compile(r"^\s*You kick at empty space\.\s*$"), # Exclude kick message
        re.compile(r"^\s*Dumb move! You strain a muscle\.\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*What a strange direction!\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*The (?:jeweled|pine) wand glows and fades\.\s*$"), # Exclude specific wand message for clarity
        re.compile(r"^\s*The lava cools and solidifies\.\s*$"), # Exclude this from game_message, as it's extracted as Environmental_change

    ]

    applyable_items_list = []
    zapable_items_list = []
    quaffable_items_list = []
    eatable_items_list = []
    putonable_items_list = []

    for line in message_content_raw.strip().split('\n'):
        line_stripped = line.strip()
        if not line_stripped:
            continue

        is_excluded = False
        for pattern in excluded_message_patterns:
            if pattern.match(line_stripped):
                is_excluded = True
                break
        
        if is_excluded:
            continue

        # Prioritize interactive prompts over parsing as general message or item
        prompt_match = interactive_prompt_pattern.match(line_stripped)
        direction_prompt_match = direction_prompt_pattern.match(line_stripped)
        if prompt_match:
            action_type = prompt_match.group(2)
            options_match = re.search(r"\[([a-z?* ]+)\]", line_stripped)
            options = options_match.group(1).strip() if options_match else "None"
            interactive_prompt_str = f"Interactive_prompt: Action required: '{action_type}'. Options: [{options}]."
            continue
        elif direction_prompt_match:
            interactive_prompt_str = "Interactive_prompt: Direction required."
            continue

        potential_items_in_line = []
        item_found_on_line = False
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line_stripped):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            item_charges = match.group(3) if match.group(3) else ""

            is_identified = bool(item_charges) or \
                            ("unidentified" not in item_description.lower() and \
                            ("wand of" in item_description.lower() or "potion of" in item_description.lower() or \
                             "scroll of" in item_description.lower() or "ring of" in item_description.lower() or \
                             "amulet of" in item_description.lower() or "spellbook of" in item_description.lower() or \
                             "dagger" in item_description.lower() or "sword" in item_description.lower() or \
                             "armor" in item_description.lower() or "shield" in item_description.lower() or \
                             "helmet" in item_description.lower() or "cloak" in item_description.lower() or \
                             "pair of gloves" in item_description.lower() or "pair of boots" in item_description.lower() or \
                             "pick-axe" in item_description.lower() or "lump of royal jelly" in item_description.lower() or \
                             "tin of spinach" in item_description.lower() or "apple" in item_description.lower() or \
                             "kebab" in item_description.lower() or "horn" in item_description.lower()))
            
            identity_status_tag = ""
            if not is_identified and "(unidentified)" not in item_description.lower():
                identity_status_tag = " (Unidentified)"
            
            full_item_description = item_description + item_charges + identity_status_tag
            
            if "horn" in item_description.lower() or "ring" in item_description.lower():
                applyable_items_list.append(f"'{item_key}': {full_item_description}")
            elif "wand" in item_description.lower() or "staff" in item_description.lower():
                zapable_items_list.append(f"'{item_key}': {full_item_description}")
            elif "potion" in item_description.lower() or "elixir" in item_description.lower():
                quaffable_items_list.append(f"'{item_key}': {full_item_description}")
            elif "food" in item_description.lower() or "apple" in item_description.lower() or "kebab" in item_description.lower() or "tin" in item_description.lower() or "jelly" in item_description.lower():
                eatable_items_list.append(f"'{item_key}': {full_item_description}")
            elif "amulet" in item_description.lower() or "cloak" in item_description.lower() or "armor" in item_description.lower() or "gloves" in item_description.lower() or "helmet" in item_description.lower() or "shield" in item_description.lower() or "boots" in item_description.lower():
                putonable_items_list.append(f"'{item_key}': {full_item_description}")

            item_entry = f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {full_item_description}."
            potential_items_in_line.append(item_entry)
            item_found_on_line = True

        if item_found_on_line:
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            general_messages_content.append(line_stripped)

    if interactive_prompt_str:
        extracted_info.append(interactive_prompt_str)

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)
        
        if applyable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Applyable_items: {'; '.join(applyable_items_list)}")
        if zapable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Zapable_items: {'; '.join(zapable_items_list)}")
        if quaffable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Quaffable_items: {'; '.join(quaffable_items_list)}")
        if eatable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Eatable_items: {'; '.join(eatable_items_list)}")
        if putonable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Putonable_items: {'; '.join(putonable_items_list)}")

        action_hints_list = []
        action_hints_list.append("For 'applyable' items (e.g., horns, rings), use 'apply <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'zapable' items (e.g., wands, staves): First use 'zap' to prompt for item choice, then provide *only* the item key as a separate action. Note: Zapping adjacent empty spaces carries reflection risk, and 'far' directions are not valid.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'quaffable' items (e.g., potions, elixirs): First use 'quaff' to prompt for item choice, then provide *only* the item key as a separate action.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'eatable' items (e.g., food, apples, kebabs), use 'eat <item key>'.; For 'putonable' items (e.g., amulets, cloaks, armor, gloves, helmets, shields, boots), use 'puton <item key>'.")
        extracted_info.append(f"ACTION HINT: {'; '.join(action_hints_list)}")

    # Add detection for environmental changes from messages
    if re.search(r"The lava cools and solidifies\.", observation_text):
        extracted_info.append("Environmental_change: One or more molten lava tiles have solidified.")

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])
    if not death_message_found and cleaned_general_message: 
        if (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
            not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
            not re.match(r"^[. ]*$", cleaned_general_message) and
            len(cleaned_general_message) > 0 # Ensure it's not an empty string after all filtering
           ):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    if not death_message_found:
        stats_match = re.search(
            r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
            observation_text
        )
        if stats_match:
            dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
            extracted_info.append(
                f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
            )
    
    return "\n".join([line for line in extracted_info if line.strip()])

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Function to calculate Manhattan distance and direction
    def manhattan_distance_and_direction(x1, y1, x2, y2):
        dx = x1 - x2
        dy = y1 - y2
        distance = abs(dx) + abs(dy)
        
        direction = []
        if dy > 0:
            direction.append("north")
        elif dy < 0:
            direction.append("south")
        
        if dx > 0:
            direction.append("west")
        elif dx < 0:
            direction.append("east")

        if not direction and distance == 0:
            return 0, "at current location"
        elif not direction: # Fallback for cardinal if no diagonal components are set (e.g. perfectly cardinal)
            if dy > 0: direction.append("north")
            elif dy < 0: direction.append("south")
            if dx > 0: direction.append("west")
            elif dx < 0: direction.append("east")

        return distance, f" to the {' '.join(direction)}" if direction else ""

    agent_x, agent_y = None, None # These will hold the *absolute* coordinates of the agent
    visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1 # Relative visual coordinates of '@' on the map section
    agent_character_class = None
    agent_current_title = None # New: to store agent's title from the status line
    is_absolute_mapping_possible = False # Flag to track if we can use absolute coordinates for map features
    death_message_found = False # Global flag for death state
    
    # Default coord_desc, will be updated if absolute mapping is possible
    coord_desc = "visual coordinates relative to map snippet origin"

    # Step 1: Attempt to extract agent's absolute position and character class from cursor line (most explicit)
    # This is considered the authoritative "character_class" as it's the class the agent _is_.
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*Yourself a (archeologist|priestess|healer|spelunker|tourist|cavewoman|knight|wizard|rogue|barbarian|elf|monk|samurai|valkyrie|dwarf|fighter|troglodyte|digger)\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_character_class = agent_pos_match.group(1)
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(3))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_character_class: {agent_character_class}. (From 'Yourself a <class>' message)")
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_map_coordinates: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}). (From 'Yourself a <class>' message)")
        is_absolute_mapping_possible = True
        coord_desc = "absolute world coordinates" # Update coord_desc here
    else: 
        # If "Yourself a <class> (x,y)" is not found, *do not* directly use generic cursor for absolute agent_x,y right away.
        # It's less reliable for *agent's* absolute position in all contexts.
        cursor_coords_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
        if cursor_coords_match:
            # Report generic cursor position, but do not use it to establish absolute agent_x,y for map features.
            cursor_x_generic = int(cursor_coords_match.group(1))
            cursor_y_generic = int(cursor_coords_match.group(2))
            extracted_info.append(f"Cursor_position (generic): ({cursor_x_generic}, {cursor_y_generic}). (This is a generic cursor, not confirmed as agent's position for absolute map features)")

    # Attempt to extract agent's current title from the status line
    status_title_match = re.search(r"Agent the (\w+)\s+St:", observation_text)
    if status_title_match:
        agent_current_title = status_title_match.group(1).lower()
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_current_title: {agent_current_title}. (From status line)")


    # Extract map information. We need this to find the visual position of '@' irrespective of Strategy 1 success.
    map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
    if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
        map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

        # Define markers for the end of the map content
        # More robust end markers, looking for double newline before status or No Points
        end_markers = []
        for marker_pattern in [
            r"\n\nAgent the \w+\s+St:", # New: double newline before status
            r"\n\n\s*St:", # New: double newline before generic status
            r"\nNo\s+Points" # Death screen marker, use single newline as it's often directly followed
        ]:
            match = re.search(marker_pattern, map_content_after_tag, re.MULTILINE)
            if match:
                end_markers.append(match.start())
        
        map_end_offset = -1
        if end_markers:
            map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
        
        map_section_raw = ""
        if map_end_offset != -1:
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
        else:
            # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

        if map_section_raw:
            map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
            
            # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
            for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if '@' in line:
                    visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                    visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                    break
            
            # Add visual agent position if found, irrespective of absolute mapping
            if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                # Add the note about visual Y coordinate potentially differing from absolute
                extracted_info.append(f"Visual agent (@) on current map snippet at relative position: ({visual_agent_x_on_map}, {visual_agent_y_on_map}). (Note: Visual Y coordinate on map snippet might differ from absolute Y coordinate due to rendering.)")
            else:
                # If agent symbol '@' isn't found, then map features can't be reliably extracted either.
                extracted_info.append("WARNING: Visual agent '@' not found on the map, map feature extraction is severely limited or impossible.")
                # Force is_absolute_mapping_possible to False here as well, since we can't align.
                is_absolute_mapping_possible = False

            # Explicitly state whether absolute mapping is used
            if not is_absolute_mapping_possible and (visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1):
                 extracted_info.append("Absolute map coordinates for features could not be reliably determined. Features will be reported with visual coordinates relative to the map snippet origin.")
            elif is_absolute_mapping_possible and (visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1):
                 extracted_info.append("All map features are reported with absolute world coordinates.")


            walls = []
            open_floor = []
            molten_lava = []
            fountains = []
            stairs_up = []
            stairs_down = []
            corridors = []
            monsters_rat = []
            doors_closed = []
            dropped_items_on_map = [] # To distinguish from inventory items
            
            monsters_within_striking_distance = []
            
            # new lists for safe/lethal moves:
            safe_adjacent_move_targets = [] 
            lethal_adjacent_move_targets = []
            
            # New list for adjacent zappable features and their danger levels
            adjacent_zappable_features = []
            
            # First visible obstacle for distant zapping - renamed for clarity as it includes adjacent
            # first_visible_zap_targets = {} # This will be built as a list of strings for clearer output
            
            # Directions for adjacent moves
            adjacent_directions = {
                (0, -1): "north", (1, -1): "northeast", (1, 0): "east", (1, 1): "southeast",
                (0, 1): "south", (-1, 1): "southwest", (-1, 0): "west", (-1, -1): "northwest"
            }
            
            immediate_move_options = {}
            adjacent_interactive_features = {}

            # Calculate map_offset only if absolute mapping is possible
            current_map_offset_x = 0
            current_map_offset_y = 0
            if is_absolute_mapping_possible and agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
                current_map_offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                current_map_offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

            # Map for looking up features
            # Storing all map characters with their determined coordinates (absolute or visual)
            map_grid_chars = {}
            map_height = len(map_lines)
            map_width = max(len(line) for line in map_lines) if map_lines else 0


            for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                    # Determine feature coordinates: absolute if mapping is possible, else visual
                    if is_absolute_mapping_possible:
                        feature_coords = (x_offset + current_map_offset_x, y_offset + current_map_offset_y)
                    else:
                        feature_coords = (x_offset, y_offset)
                    map_grid_chars[feature_coords] = char # Store chars based on their determined coordinates

                    # Determine if current char is where the agent is (either absolutely or visually if only visual is known)
                    is_agent_square = (x_offset == visual_agent_x_on_map and y_offset == visual_agent_y_on_map)

                    # Determine if adjacent to agent (only if visual agent position is known)
                    if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                        is_adjacent_char = (abs(visual_agent_x_on_map - x_offset) <= 1 and abs(visual_agent_y_on_map - y_offset) <= 1 and not is_agent_square)
                    else:
                        is_adjacent_char = False

                    feature_type = "unknown" # Default type, should be refined

                    if char == '|' or char == '-': # walls (horizontal or vertical)
                        walls.append(feature_coords)
                        feature_type = "wall"
                    elif char == '.':
                        open_floor.append(feature_coords)
                        feature_type = "open floor"
                    elif char == '}': # Molten lava or open door
                        molten_lava.append(feature_coords)
                        feature_type = "molten lava" # Set as molten lava by default for '}'
                    elif char == 'F': 
                        fountains.append(feature_coords)
                        feature_type = "fountain"
                    elif char == '<': 
                        stairs_up.append(feature_coords)
                        feature_type = "stairs up"
                    elif char == '>': 
                        stairs_down.append(feature_coords)
                        feature_type = "stairs down"
                    elif char == ':': 
                        corridors.append(feature_coords)
                        feature_type = "corridor"
                    elif char == 'r': 
                        monsters_rat.append(feature_coords)
                        feature_type = "rat monster"
                    elif char == '+':
                        doors_closed.append(feature_coords)
                        feature_type = "closed door"
                    elif re.match(r"[a-z]", char) and char != '@': # Assuming single lowercase letters are dropped items, and not agent
                        dropped_items_on_map.append((feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], char))
                        feature_type = f"dropped item '{char}'"
                    elif char == ' ': # empty space outside known areas
                        feature_type = "empty space"
                    # Any other characters not explicitly defined would remain 'unknown'

                    if is_adjacent_char:
                        dx = x_offset - visual_agent_x_on_map
                        dy = y_offset - visual_agent_y_on_map
                        move_dir = adjacent_directions.get((dx, dy))

                        if move_dir:
                            # Special handling for molten lava or other lethal features regardless of general classification (e.g. open floor)
                            is_lethal = False
                            feature_type_for_interact = feature_type # Default to general feature type

                            # Check if the current feature (char) is one we know to be lethal
                            if char == '}': # The map character '}' is identified as lethal as per world knowledge
                                is_lethal = True
                                feature_type_for_interact = "molten lava"
                                
                                # Add to adjacent zappable features list with danger assessment
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - molten lava) (safe solidification)"
                                ) # New line for zappable info

                            # The `.` open floor tile can also be lethal if zapped into due to reflection,
                            # so assess it here for zappable features. Note: This is *not* for immediate movement.
                            elif char in ('.', ':', 'F', '<', '>'): # Reflective surfaces
                                # This is for classifying for `zap` action, not for *movement* lethality
                                current_surface_type = ""
                                if char == '.': current_surface_type = "open floor"
                                elif char == ':': current_surface_type = "corridor"
                                elif char == 'F': current_surface_type = "fountain"
                                elif char == '<': current_surface_type = "stairs up"
                                elif char == '>': current_surface_type = "stairs down"
                                
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - {current_surface_type}) (lethal reflection risk)"
                                )
                            elif re.match(r"[a-z]", char) and char != '@': # Dropped items are also reflective
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - dropped item) (lethal reflection risk)"
                                )
                            elif char == '|' or char == '-': # Walls
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - wall) (no reflection risk)"
                                )
                            elif char == '+': # Closed doors
                                adjacent_zappable_features.append(
                                    f"{move_dir}: ({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char} - closed door) (no reflection risk)"
                                )
                            
                            if is_lethal: # Molten lava takes precedence
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"LETHAL ({feature_type_for_interact})", feature_coords)
                                lethal_adjacent_move_targets.append(feature_coords)
                                # For interactive features, specify zappability for molten lava
                                if char == '}':
                                    adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], "molten lava (lethal, zappable with cold wand, safe solidification)")
                                else:
                                    adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], feature_type_for_interact)
                            elif char == 'r':
                                monsters_within_striking_distance.append((feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], 'rat'))
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"ATTACK (rat monster)", feature_coords)
                                adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], "rat monster") # For interaction detail
                            elif char == '|' or char == '-': # Walls are impassable for movement
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"BLOCKED (wall)", feature_coords)
                                adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], "wall") # For interaction detail
                            elif char == '+': # Closed doors are impassable for movement (until opened)
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"BLOCKED (closed door)", feature_coords)
                                adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], "closed door") # For interaction detail
                            else: # Default safe for other passable terrain features like floor, corridor, stairs, fountain, and explicitly OPEN DOORS
                                if move_dir not in immediate_move_options: # Prioritize more specific statuses
                                    # Use the refined feature_type from above
                                    if char == '<': feature_type_for_interact = "stairs up"
                                    elif char == '>': feature_type_for_interact = "stairs down"
                                    elif char == '.': feature_type_for_interact = "open floor"
                                    elif char == 'F': feature_type_for_interact = "fountain"
                                    elif char == ':': feature_type_for_interact = "corridor"
                                    elif re.match(r"[a-z]", char) and char != '@': feature_type_for_interact = f"dropped item '{char}'"
                                    else: feature_type_for_interact = "open space" # fallback
                                    
                                    immediate_move_options[move_dir] = (f"SAFE ({feature_type_for_interact})", feature_coords)
                                    safe_adjacent_move_targets.append(feature_coords) # Moved this here to ensure it's only for SAFE moves
                                    adjacent_interactive_features[move_dir] = (feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1], feature_type_for_interact)
            
            # --- First Visible Zap Targets Logic Start ---
            # Only calculate if the agent's visual position on the map is known
            if visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                # Iterate through directions: N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW
                # The dx, dy tuples here correspond to (offset_x, offset_y) from agent's visual position
                direction_vectors = {
                    "north": (0, -1), "northeast": (1, -1), "east": (1, 0), "southeast": (1, 1),
                    "south": (0, 1), "southwest": (-1, 1), "west": (-1, 0), "northwest": (-1, -1)
                }
                
                temp_zap_obstacles = []
                # agent_central_coords (absolute or visual depending on is_absolute_mapping_possible)
                agent_central_coords = (agent_x, agent_y) if is_absolute_mapping_possible else (visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map)

                for dir_name, (dx_dir, dy_dir) in direction_vectors.items():
                    current_steps = 1
                    first_obstacle_info = None # (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, dist_manhattan)
                    beam_path_description = [] # To store details of what the beam passes through

                    while True:
                        check_visual_x = visual_agent_x_on_map + dx_dir * current_steps
                        check_visual_y = visual_agent_y_on_map + dy_dir * current_steps

                        # Check if within map bounds of the current snippet
                        if not (0 <= check_visual_y < map_height and 
                                0 <= check_visual_x < map_width and 
                                check_visual_y < len(map_lines) and 
                                check_visual_x < len(map_lines[check_visual_y])): 
                            # If out of map bounds, and no obstacle found, the beam goes into void, which is safe.
                            if not first_obstacle_info: # Only if nothing was hit yet, then the target is 'empty space (void)'
                                feature_coords_for_void = (check_visual_x + current_map_offset_x, check_visual_y + current_map_offset_y) if is_absolute_mapping_possible else (check_visual_x, check_visual_y)
                                current_dist, _ = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_central_coords[0], agent_central_coords[1], feature_coords_for_void[0], feature_coords_for_void[1])
                                # Use ' ' as char_at_pos for empty space (void)
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords_for_void, ' ', "empty space (void)", "no reflection risk", current_dist)
                            break # Out of bounds of the current map snippet or line too short

                        char_at_pos = map_lines[check_visual_y][check_visual_x]

                        # Determine feature coords (absolute or visual)
                        if is_absolute_mapping_possible:
                            feature_coords = (check_visual_x + current_map_offset_x, check_visual_y + current_map_offset_y)
                        else:
                            feature_coords = (check_visual_x, check_visual_y)

                        # Manhattan distance
                        distance_to_feature_manhattan, _ = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_central_coords[0], agent_central_coords[1], feature_coords[0], feature_coords[1])
                        
                        obstacle_type = "unknown"
                        zap_danger = "unknown"
                        
                        # Categorize the feature for beam interaction
                        if char_at_pos == '}':
                            obstacle_type = "molten lava"
                            zap_danger = "safe solidification"
                            if not first_obstacle_info:
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, distance_to_feature_manhattan)
                            break # Lava is always the primary target if encountered
                        elif char_at_pos in ('|', '-', '#', '+'): # Walls, corridor walls, closed doors are solid obstacles
                            obstacle_type = "solid obstacle (wall/closed door/corridor wall)"
                            zap_danger = "no reflection risk" # Safe to zap at solid objects
                            if not first_obstacle_info:
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, distance_to_feature_manhattan)
                            break # Solid objects block beam
                        elif char_at_pos in ('.', ':', 'F', '<', '>') or (re.match(r"[a-z]", char_at_pos) and char_at_pos not in ('@', 'r')): # Reflective surfaces, exlcuding agent and rat monsters
                            # Record what the beam passes through, but don't stop unless it's the first obstacle
                            current_surface_type = ""
                            if char_at_pos == '.': current_surface_type = "open floor"
                            elif char_at_pos == ':': current_surface_type = "corridor"
                            elif char_at_pos == 'F': current_surface_type = "fountain"
                            elif char_at_pos == '<': current_surface_type = "stairs up"
                            elif char_at_pos == '>': current_surface_type = "stairs down"
                            elif (re.match(r"[a-z]", char_at_pos) and char_at_pos != '@'): current_surface_type = "dropped item"
                            else: current_surface_type = "reflective surface" #fallback
                            
                            beam_path_description.append(f"({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char_at_pos} - {current_surface_type})")

                            if not first_obstacle_info: # If a reflective surface is the *first* thing hit
                                obstacle_type = current_surface_type # Set to the specific surface type
                                # Determine lethal reflection risk based on adjacency
                                zap_danger = "lethal reflection risk" if distance_to_feature_manhattan == 1 else "reflection risk (not immediately lethal)"
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, distance_to_feature_manhattan)
                                # For a purely reflective surface as target, the beam stops and reflects.
                                break 
                        elif char_at_pos == 'r': # Monsters are also reflective
                            obstacle_type = "rat monster"
                            zap_danger = "reflection risk (not immediately lethal)" # Mosters reflect but not immediately lethal, as per standard NetHack mechanics (can hit other targets, but not player directly from a single reflection)
                            if not first_obstacle_info:
                                first_obstacle_info = (feature_coords, char_at_pos, obstacle_type, zap_danger, distance_to_feature_manhattan)
                            break
                        elif char_at_pos == ' ': # Empty space / void within visible area, non-reflective and doesn't stop beam
                            beam_path_description.append(f"({feature_coords[0]}, {feature_coords[1]}) ({char_at_pos} - empty space)")
                            # If the beam reaches beyond the map or hits nothing, the 'void' is the target.
                            # We continue through empty space until a solid or reflective surface, or map edge.
                        
                        current_steps += 1
                        
                        if current_steps > max(map_height, map_width) + 2: # Heuristic to prevent infinite loops for edge cases
                             break

                    if first_obstacle_info:
                        f_coords, f_char, f_type, f_danger, f_dist = first_obstacle_info
                        desc = f"{dir_name}: {f_coords} ({f_char} - {f_type}) ({f_danger}, distance: {f_dist} steps)"
                        if beam_path_description:
                            # Filter out the final target from beam_path_description if it was added.
                            # The 'first_obstacle_info' already contains the terminal target.
                            # We only want to list intermediate reflective surfaces.
                            # Also filter out the STARTING point for the description. The first item in beam_path_description can be the target itself if it's adjacent.
                            final_target_str_part = f"({f_coords[0]}, {f_coords[1]}) ({f_char}"
                            # Filter out any path descriptions that are identical to the first obstacle info
                            filtered_beam_path = [
                                path_desc for path_desc in beam_path_description 
                                if not (final_target_str_part in path_desc and f_type in path_desc)
                            ]
                            
                            if filtered_beam_path:
                                desc += f" (Beam passes through {'; '.join(filtered_beam_path)} without reflection.)"
                        temp_zap_obstacles.append(desc)
            
            # Sort the distant zap obstacles for consistent output
            if temp_zap_obstacles:
                sorted_zap_targets = []
                direction_order_keys = ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]
                # Use the map coordinates or visual coordinates prefix once for the entire list
                prefix = f"First_visible_zap_targets (all coordinates are {coord_desc} - direction: (x,y) (char - type) (zap danger, distance)):"
                
                for d_key in direction_order_keys:
                    for item_str in temp_zap_obstacles:
                        if item_str.startswith(f"{d_key}:"):

            if sorted_move_options_str:

            # Adjacent interactive features for context or specific actions (e.g., kick, open)
            if adjacent_interactive_features:
                sorted_features_list = []
                # Keep consistent direction ordering
                for move_dir in ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]:
                    if move_dir in adjacent_interactive_features:

                        sorted_features_list.append(f"{move_dir}: ({fx}, {fy}) - {ftype}")
                if sorted_features_list: # Ensure it's not empty after filtering
                    extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent interactive features (all coordinates are {coord_desc} - direction: (x,y) - type): {'; '.join(sorted_features_list)}.")

            # Add newly extracted adjacent zappable features
            if adjacent_zappable_features:
                # Sort by direction for consistent output order
                # Use a specific order for directions
                direction_order = ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]
                sorted_zappable_features = []
                # Iterate through preferred order and add to sorted list
                for d_ord in direction_order:
                    for feature_str in adjacent_zappable_features:
                        if feature_str.startswith(f"{d_ord}:"):
                            # The coord_desc is already included in the `prefix`
                            sorted_zappable_features.append(feature_str)
                # Remove duplicates while maintaining order (Python 3.7+ feature)
                sorted_zappable_features = list(dict.fromkeys(sorted_zappable_features))
                
                if sorted_zappable_features: # Ensure it's not empty, even after sorting
                    extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent zappable features (all coordinates are {coord_desc} - direction: (x,y) (char - type) (zap danger)): {'; '.join(sorted_zappable_features)}.")
                
    # Extract system feedback messages (e.g., "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry")

    # Extract all generic messages first, then filter for inventory and prompts

    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1).strip()
    else:
        message_content_raw = "" 
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    interactive_prompt_str = None 
    
    # Check for death message again, independently of map context.
    death_message_pattern = re.compile(

        re.DOTALL
    )
    death_message_match = death_message_pattern.search(observation_text)

    if death_message_match:
        cause_of_death = death_message_match.group(1).strip()
        extracted_info.append(f"Death_message: Agent died. Cause: {cause_of_death}.")
        death_message_found = True
        agent_character_class = None # Clear class on death
        agent_current_title = None # Clear title on death
    else:
        general_death_message_match = re.search(r"(?:No\s+Points\s+Name.*?)\n(.*?died in.*?\.)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
        if general_death_message_match:
             extracted_info.append(f"Death_message: {general_death_message_match.group(1).strip()}.")
             death_message_found = True
             agent_character_class = None # Clear class on death
             agent_current_title = None # Clear title on death

    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(\s+\(\d+:\d+\))?(?=\.\s\s|\.$|\n|$)")
    interactive_prompt_pattern = re.compile(r"^(What do you want to ([a-z]+)\? \[[a-z?* ]+\])$", re.IGNORECASE)
    direction_prompt_pattern = re.compile(r"^In what direction\?$")

    # Patterns to identify lines that should be excluded from general messages if a death occurred
    excluded_message_patterns = [
        re.compile(r"Your previous output did not contain a valid action\. Retry"),

        direction_prompt_pattern,
        re.compile(r"^\s*No\s+Points\s+Name\s+Hp\s+\[max\]\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*\d+\s+Agent-.*?died in The Dungeons of Doom on$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*level \d+\.\s+.*? Burned by molten lava\.\s+- \[(\d+)\]\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*level \d+\.\s+.*? Killed by a bolt of cold\.\s+- \[(\d+)\]\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*level \d+\.\s+.*? died in The Dungeons of Doom on\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*You made the top ten list!\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*Hp \[max\]\s*$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*\d+\s+Agent-\w+-\w+-\w+-\w+ died in The Dungeons of Doom on$"),
        re.compile(r"^\s*--More--\s*$"), # Exclude --More--

    ]

    eatable_items_list = []

    for line in message_content_raw.strip().split('\n'):

                break

        # Prioritize interactive prompts over parsing as general message or item
        prompt_match = interactive_prompt_pattern.match(line_stripped)

            identity_status_tag = ""

                identity_status_tag = " (Unidentified)"
            
            full_item_description = item_description + item_charges + identity_status_tag
            
            if "horn" in item_description.lower() or "ring" in item_description.lower():
                applyable_items_list.append(f"'{item_key}': {full_item_description}")
            elif "wand" in item_description.lower() or "staff" in item_description.lower():

            elif "potion" in item_description.lower() or "elixir" in item_description.lower():

                eatable_items_list.append(f"'{item_key}': {full_item_description}")
            elif "amulet" in item_description.lower() or "cloak" in item_description.lower() or "armor" in item_description.lower() or "gloves" in item_description.lower() or "helmet" in item_description.lower() or "shield" in item_description.lower() or "boots" in item_description.lower():
                putonable_items_list.append(f"'{item_key}': {full_item_description}")

            item_entry = f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {full_item_description}."
            potential_items_in_line.append(item_entry)
            item_found_on_line = True

        if item_found_on_line:
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            general_messages_content.append(line_stripped)

    if interactive_prompt_str:
        extracted_info.append(interactive_prompt_str)

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)
        
        if applyable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Applyable_items: {'; '.join(applyable_items_list)}")
        if zapable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Zapable_items: {'; '.join(zapable_items_list)}")
        if quaffable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Quaffable_items: {'; '.join(quaffable_items_list)}")
        if eatable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Eatable_items: {'; '.join(eatable_items_list)}")
        if putonable_items_list:
            extracted_info.append(f"Putonable_items: {'; '.join(putonable_items_list)}")

        action_hints_list = []
        action_hints_list.append("For 'applyable' items (e.g., horns, rings), use 'apply <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'zapable' items (e.g., wands, staves): First use 'zap' to prompt for item choice, then provide *only* the item key as a separate action. Note: Zapping adjacent empty spaces carries reflection risk, and 'far' directions are not valid.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'quaffable' items (e.g., potions, elixirs): First use 'quaff' to prompt for item choice, then provide *only* the item key as a separate action.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'eatable' items (e.g., food, apples, kebabs), use 'eat <item key>'.; For 'putonable' items (e.g., amulets, cloaks, armor, gloves, helmets, shields, boots), use 'puton <item key>'.")
        extracted_info.append(f"ACTION HINT: {'; '.join(action_hints_list)}")

    # Add detection for environmental changes from messages
    if re.search(r"The lava cools and solidifies\.", observation_text):
        extracted_info.append("Environmental_change: One or more molten lava tiles have solidified.")

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])
    if not death_message_found and cleaned_general_message: 
        if (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
            not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
            not re.match(r"^[. ]*$", cleaned_general_message) and
            len(cleaned_general_message) > 0 # Ensure it's not an empty string after all filtering
           ):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    if not death_message_found:
        stats_match = re.search(
            r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
            observation_text
        )
        if stats_match:
            dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
            extracted_info.append(
                f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
            )
    
    return "\n".join([line for line in extracted_info if line.strip()])
